ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for bun nem nuong. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Noodles, cooked) used as closest parent; no specific code for Vietnamese dry vermicelli with grilled pork sausage.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for this specific Vietnamese noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of bun nem nuong is approximately 380 g, comprising a bowl of dry rice vermicelli topped with several pieces of grilled pork sausage, fresh herbs, bean sprouts, and a side of fish sauce dipping sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Bun Thit Nuong (Vietnamese Grilled Pork Noodle Bowl) — component-based estimation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked rice vermicelli, grilled pork sausage, fresh herbs, and fish sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for bun nem nuong exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 55% cooked rice vermicelli (USDA FDC #169726), 30% grilled pork sausage/nem nuong (USDA FDC #167903 pork sausage grilled), 10% fresh vegetables and herbs, 5% fish sauce dipping sauce. Values represent per-100g of the assembled dish. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — rice vermicelli / rice noodles, cooked
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bun nem nuong as a composite dish. GI of 53 is based on the dominant carbohydrate component: cooked rice vermicelli / rice noodles, which has a published GI of approximately 53 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr). The protein and fat from grilled pork sausage will further attenuate the postprandial glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (53 × 70.3 g available carbohydrate per 380 g serving) / 100 = approximately 37.3, rounded to 37.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from fish sauce and pork sausage seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Each serving of bun nem nuong provides approximately 1,216 mg sodium (320 mg per 100 g × 380 g), representing over 50% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced fish sauce and limit dipping sauce volume.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from rice vermicelli carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 53 (low) but a glycemic load of approximately 37 per serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a smaller portion (200–250 g) and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from grilled pork sausage and fish sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content (~1.6 g per 100 g) from pork sausage and the high sodium load from fish sauce are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and choose leaner protein alternatives where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bun nem nuong is a Vietnamese dry noodle dish consisting of thin rice vermicelli (bun) served at room temperature or slightly warm, topped with slices or rolls of nem nuong — a fragrant grilled pork sausage made from minced pork seasoned with garlic, fish sauce, sugar, and black pepper, then shaped and charcoal-grilled or pan-grilled until caramelised on the outside. The dish is typically garnished with fresh herbs such as mint, perilla, and Vietnamese coriander, shredded lettuce, bean sprouts, and crushed roasted peanuts, and served with a nuoc cham dipping sauce (fish sauce, lime juice, sugar, garlic, and chilli). In Singapore, it is served at Vietnamese restaurants as a light main course. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (~148 kcal per 100 g), provides a meaningful protein contribution from the pork sausage (~8 g per 100 g), and derives its carbohydrate primarily from rice vermicelli (GI ~53, low). The sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce and sausage seasoning, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per standard serving (~380 g) is approximately 37, which is high in absolute terms despite the low GI of the noodle base, and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate serving size accordingly.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for bun nem nuong exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked rice vermicelli and grilled pork sausage. GI is estimated from the analogous rice vermicelli GI value from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. All other -1 values reflect absence of data in the estimation sources rather than confirmed zero. No core macro is zero; all four core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are positive and nutritionally plausible for this dish. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on cooked rice vermicelli as the dominant carbohydrate source per University of Sydney GI Database. Sodium is elevated due to fish sauce and pork sausage seasoning; this is clinically relevant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The dish is not halal as it contains pork. Allergen note: fish sauce contains fish; soy sauce may be present in nem nuong marinade introducing gluten risk.
